name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is a large payday and installment lender offering short-term cash loans of $100–$2,000 at high APRs across 800+ stores in ~28 states.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America is one of the largest non-bank consumer lenders in the United States. The company was acquired in April 2012 by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican retail and financial conglomerate, for approximately $780 million. It holds state-licensed lending authority in roughly 27–28 states, carries a Great Place to Work employee certification, and received BBB accreditation and an A+ rating in 2024. It is not a nonprofit, credit union, or CDFI — it is a for-profit, high-cost short-term lender.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan — amounts from $100 to $2,000, repayment terms of 7 to 30 days, with typical APRs of 350%–700%. It also offers installment loans (3–12 month terms, APRs of 200%–348%+), title loans secured by a vehicle, and revolving lines of credit up to $4,000. Check cashing is available at physical locations. In-store applicants can receive cash in approximately 30 minutes; online applicants typically see funding within 24 hours. Fees and eligibility thresholds vary by state and are disclosed after the application is submitted, not upfront.

With an estimated 800–1,500+ physical stores operating in approximately 27 states, Advance America maintains one of the largest brick-and-mortar footprints of any non-bank consumer lender in the country. Its online portal (online.advanceamerica.net) provides full account management — loan applications, payment scheduling, live chat support — protected by multi-factor authentication. The company's Great Place to Work certification stands out in an industry often associated with high employee turnover, and its sheer scale means consistent product availability and in-person service in markets underserved by traditional banks.

The central limitation of Advance America's products is their cost. APRs of 350%–700% on payday loans are among the highest in consumer lending and are documented pathways to debt cycles for borrowers who roll over or reborrow. The company's regulatory history includes an $18.5 million CFPB enforcement penalty in 2012 and over 1,066 consumer complaints filed with the CFPB since 2013. Its BBB customer review score is 1.7 out of 5 — far below its formal A+ business rating — with 302 complaints filed in the past three years. For anyone who qualifies for a personal loan, credit union PAL, or employer cash advance program, those options will almost always carry dramatically lower costs.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee found. Short-term loan fees are non-refundable once funds are disbursed.

tiers.name: Payday Loans
features: Fees vary by state — disclosed post-application

tiers.name: Installment Loans
features: Fees vary by state — disclosed post-application

tiers.name: Title Loans
features: Vehicle must be owned outright or nearly paid off

pros: One of the largest payday lenders in the US with 800–1,500+ physical stores in ~28 states
In-store cash disbursement in approximately 30 minutes — among the fastest in the industry
Online applications available in 27 states with funding typically within 24 hours
BBB A+ rating with formal accreditation since 2024
Nearly 30 years of operation since founding in 1997
Secure online portal with MFA, payment scheduling, and live chat support
Both in-store and online channels give borrowers flexibility on how to apply and receive funds
cons: Payday loan APRs of 350%–700% are among the highest in consumer lending and carry serious debt-cycle risk
CFPB levied an $18.5 million enforcement penalty against the company in 2012
Over 1,066 CFPB consumer complaints filed since 2013
BBB customer review score is 1.7 out of 5 (154 reviews) — far below its formal A+ business rating
Loan fees and eligibility terms are not disclosed upfront — only revealed after application submission
best_for: Consumers facing a short-term cash emergency who have no access to bank credit, credit cards, or lower-cost alternatives
Borrowers who need in-person service and same-day cash from a physical store location
People in one of the ~28 states where Advance America operates who need funds within 24 hours
Individuals between paychecks with a clear, specific repayment plan for a small short-term loan
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers who need emergency cash quickly and have exhausted lower-cost options such as personal loans, credit union PALs, or employer advances. The extremely high APRs — up to 700% on payday loans — make these products genuinely expensive and potentially harmful if rolled over or reborrowed. Borrowers should treat Advance America as a last resort, borrow only what they can repay on the next due date, and avoid using it for recurring shortfalls.
services: Installment Loans (multi-month repayment, varies by state)
Online account management portal with MFA
Loan status tracking and account history

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
